layout_observations: Comparison between the original PROYA retail display image and its multi-class semantic segmentation breakdown.
The segmentation masks successfully isolate key visual components including the model, product display shelf, text, and background.
The yellow mask isolates the main model graphic on the front counter wrap.
The green mask isolates the product tester display area on top of the counter.
The visual hierarchy is dominated by the large blue counter wrap and the tall vertical roll-up banner.
Color contrast between the white text and blue background provides excellent legibility for the primary brand message.
Strong brand consistency with a unified blue, white, and orange color palette.
Highly effective segmentation of the primary promotional model and product display.
implementation_constraints: Use the isolated masks to train or validate retail shelf-monitoring and compliance algorithms.
Ensure edge-refinement techniques are applied to the green mask to better capture transparent and reflective cosmetic bottles.
Increase the font size and contrast of secondary promotional labels on the counter.
Apply a bilateral filter or edge-detection pre-processing step to improve mask boundaries around glass bottles.
warnings: Vision Banana endpoint missing; used palette-mask fallback.
Some small promotional text labels on the counter are omitted or merged into larger masks.
Ambiguity in whether the background store elements are fully excluded from the primary analysis masks.
Low legibility of small promotional text labels on the counter shelf.
Reflective surfaces on product packaging cause slight boundary blurring in segmentation.
The contact sheet provides low-resolution previews, limiting the ability to assess pixel-level mask accuracy.
No ground-truth labels or class names are explicitly provided for the color-coded masks.
